title: [Reel] “Looking expensive is not the same as looking credible.”
description:
Content: Personal Branding | Scripts: HOOK (0–4 sec)

Text on screen:
“Looking expensive is not the same as looking credible.”

Voiceover:
“And some of the smartest founders in the world understood that very early.”

BUILD (4–12 sec)

Voiceover:
“Take Steve Jobs for example.

The black turtleneck was never about fashion.
It was about consistency, clarity, and recognisability.

Even Mark Zuckerberg kept things visually simple —
because strong presence rarely needs visual noise.”

EDUCATION (12–24 sec)

Voiceover:
“Most young founders today confuse luxury with authority.

But credibility is built differently.

Through fit.
Grooming.
Composure.
Consistency.
And how confidently you carry yourself in a room.”

DEEPER INSIGHT (24–33 sec)

Voiceover:
“Because people don’t just read your clothes.
They read your self-perception.

And whether you realise it or not,
your appearance is communicating something before you speak.”

ENDING / CTA (33–40 sec)

Text on screen:
“Presence is perception.”

Voiceover:
“The strongest founder brands feel intentional —
not performative.

If you’re building your personal brand,
feel free to DM Mellow.”

title: [Reel] Henny to provide a celebrity content

title: [Reel] REEL 2 — "The Quiet ones"
description:
Content: Confidence | Scripts: HOOK (0–3 sec)
The most confident person in the room
is rarely the loudest.

BODY (3–22 sec)
Real confidence is quiet.
It's the person who walks in
without needing everyone to notice.
It's the person who disagrees
without needing to win.
It's the person who fails
and still shows up the next morning.
That kind of confidence —
it's not built in a gym.
It's not built on likes.
It's built by keeping promises to yourself
when nobody's watching.
One small habit.
One kept word.
One honest decision — repeated daily.
CLOSE (22–30 sec)
You don't need to be the loudest.
You need to be the most grounded.

title: [Reel] The Confidence Habit Nobody Talks About
description:
Content: Confidence | Scripts: Client: Henny, what’s the fastest way to build confidence?

Henny walks in with a mic:

“Start keeping small promises to yourself.”

Because confidence grows
when you start trusting your own word.

Wake up when you said you would.
Finish what you started.
Stay consistent with small habits.

Most people keep disappointing themselves quietly
and then wonder why they feel disconnected.

Self-trust creates self-confidence.

And real confidence?

It’s not built overnight.

It’s built through small actions repeated honestly. | Link: info.docx

title: [Reel] Dressing tips for a lean body type with slight belly out
description:
Content: Filler | Idea: LINK

https://www.instagram.com/reels/DYM9l7mSW2p/ | Scripts: HOOK (0–3 sec)
Text on screen: "Lean. Slight belly. Senior role?" (Watch the full video) 

Visual: Mirror shot — man checking himself before stepping out

Voiceover:  Not fat. Not ripped. Just a slight belly? Let us help you with the right styling  

OCCASION 1 — Casual Party / Rooftop (3–10 sec)

Text on screen: Rooftop. Lounge.

Visual: Hand clicks — dark olive overshirt, black slim chinos, white sneakers.

Voiceover: "Going for a rooftop lounge party? Open overshirt over a solid tee. Fits the vibe perfectly — because the vertical layer keeps you relaxed. And the belly? Completely unbothered."

OCCASION 2 — Date Night (10–17 sec)

Text on screen: Date Night.

Visual: Hand clicks — burgundy resort shirt, charcoal trousers, tan Chelsea boots.

Voiceover: "Got a date tonight? A half-tucked resort shirt is your answer. Looks structured and intentional — and the tuck pulls all attention up. Away from the middle, straight to the personality."

OCCASION 3 — Office (17–23 sec)

Text on screen: Boardroom.

Visual: Hand clicks — navy blazer, white shirt, dark charcoal trousers, black Oxfords.

Voiceover: "Walking into the boardroom? Blazer on. It broadens your shoulders instantly — and when shoulders lead, the belly never even enters the conversation."

OCCASION 4 — Presentation (23–28 sec)

Text on screen: The Room That Matters.

Visual: Hand clicks — navy suit, white shirt, teal tie, black Oxfords.

Voiceover: "Biggest presentation of the quarter? Fitted suit, clean shirt, sharp tie. The room forms its opinion in 3 seconds — make sure it's the right one."

CTA (28–30 sec)

Text on screen: "Dress like the Personality they remember."

Visual: Full outfit flat lay or final look

Voiceover:  follow Mellow for more such styling hacks

title: [Reel] Which founder got its Funding ?
description:
Content: Personal Branding | Scripts: VISUAL HOOK (0–3 sec)
Split screen — two founders standing side by side.

Founder A — Left

Wrinkled shirt. Loosely fitted trousers. Ungroomed hair. Slouched posture. Laptop bag hanging off one shoulder. Nervous energy — fidgeting with his phone.

Founder B — Right

Well-fitted navy blazer. Clean white shirt. Tailored trousers. Minimal watch. Groomed hair. Standing straight. Calm. Intentional. Owns the space he's standing in.
Text on screen:
"Same idea."
"One got funded."
"Why?"

HENNY ENTERS (3–8 sec)

Voiceover:
It was never about the numbers.
It was about the person presenting them.
_______________________________________________
BUILD (8–18 sec)
Investors don't just evaluate your business.
They evaluate you.
Your confidence. Your clarity. Your appearance.
The way you walk into a room —
that decision is made before you speak.
The smartest founders know this.
And they prepare for it — four ways.

THE 4 PILLARS (18–26 sec)
One — Own your visual identity.
Your photo, your wardrobe, your feed — all saying the same thing.
Two — Communicate authority.
Not aggression. Not desperation. Quiet confidence.
Three — Have a signature presence.
Online and offline — you should be instantly recognisable.
Four — Look investable.
Because perception is reality every single time

CTA (26–30 sec)
Your next investor will judge you
before you finish your first sentence.

Are you presenting yourself like someone worth betting on?
Most founders aren't — and that's exactly why Mellow exists.
Hit the DM.

title: [Carousel] The Confidence Blueprint
description:
Content: Confidence | Idea: https://www.instagram.com/p/DTSVS5eCNFo/?igsh=MXR5N2ZxbzZkczF6dg== | Scripts: Slide 1 — Hook
Still Faking It?
Real confidence isn't performed — it's built from the inside out.

Slide 2
Face Yourself.
Most people avoid this step — it's where confidence actually begins.

Slide 3
Pick Your Person.
Set goals for who you want to become — not who others expect you to be.

Slide 4
Start Anyway.
Confidence doesn't come before action. It comes because of it.

Slide 5
Trust Yourself.
Every small promise you keep to yourself rebuilds what comparison broke.

Slide 6
Watch Your Mouth.
The way you speak to yourself daily — becomes you eventually.

CTA
Build It Deliberately.
Follow Mellow for more insights on becoming the best version of yourself.

title: [Reel] Celebrity Content for Rashmika Mandhana
description:
Content: Celebrity Content | Scripts: HOOK (0–3 sec)
Text: "Same girl. Totally different energy. Why?"
VO: "Rashmika figured out something most people never do 

BEFORE (3–10 sec)
Text: "2016–2020"
VO: "She used to dress to be liked — florals, bright colours, soft curls. Warm, youthful, the girl everyone adored.."

THE SHIFT (10–16 sec)
Text: "Then the rooms got bigger."
VO: "But as the rooms got bigger, one question changed everything — from 'how do I look cute?' to 'how do I want to be perceived?'

AFTER (16–24 sec)
Text: "2022–Now"
VO:And she realised — her warmth was already loud enough. The clothes didn't need to perform anymore.

IMPACT 
Text: "Style evolves with identity."
VO: "So she got quieter. Structured sarees, soft whites, minimal accessories. And that stillness? It became her power.

CTA
That's what happens when your styling finally catches up to your growth. Follow Mellow for more."

title: [Carousel] Client case study
description:
Content: Case study | Idea: No reference | Scripts: Slide 1
"Pool party. HNI crowd. Standing in front of her wardrobe, completely blank.
The challenge was never the budget. It was finding what truly worked for her."

Slide 2 — The Challenge
"Triangular body structure. Acne-prone skin.
Every choice needed balance, softness, and ease."

Slide 3 — The Approach
"So we focused on silhouettes that flatter naturally.
Colours and fabrics that feel effortless — not forced."

Slide 4 — The Process
"That meant exploring designer stores across Fort, Mumbai.
Precision over compromise — always."

Slide 5 — The Result
"She walked in elegant, confident, and completely herself.
The wardrobe blank? Gone."

Slide 6 —
"If you've never felt truly understood while getting dressed 
DM us & we will figure it out ." | Link: info.docx

assigned_to:

Mohit

description:
Content: Competition specific | Scripts: VEX IQ

Slide 1: How problem-solving at age 7 shapes how they handle pressure at 17

Slide 2:
A stuck robot teaches one thing early.
That Problems are solvable.

Slide 3
That becomes his first reflex.
Not panic. Think.

Slide 4
Failing and rebuilding — ten times over.
Teaches him to reset, not quit.

Slide 5
He stops waiting for permission to try again.
That's self-direction. Built at 7.

Slide 6
At 17 — exams, pressure, high stakes.
Feels familiar. Not paralyzing.

Slide 7 — CTA
The window to build this is now.
Book a free session at your nearest OMOTEC branch.
